AEGIS
Constitution Principles Execution Protocols Version
Operational Specification
Audit as Completion Condition
Aegis Foundational Maxim™ Capability without constraint is not intelligence™
Specification Scope
Spec Node
  • Definition
  • Normative Requirement
  • Minimum Audit Schema
  • Validation Rules
  • Failure Conditions
  • Implementation Mapping
  • Governance Implications

1. Definition

Within Aegis, a task is not considered complete upon production of output. Completion occurs only when the output is accompanied by a valid, durable audit artifact.

“Audit as Completion Condition” means that execution lifecycle termination is dependent upon successful generation and validation of an audit record.

Output without audit is considered non-canonical.

2. Normative Requirement

For any execution above Threat Level 0, the system MUST generate a structured audit record prior to declaring completion.

The audit record MUST:

  • Bind to authority context
  • Bind to threat posture
  • Reference the active doctrine version
  • Enumerate tools invoked
  • Record artifacts produced
  • Capture state delta

If audit generation fails, execution is incomplete regardless of output validity.

3. Minimum Audit Schema

At minimum, an audit artifact SHALL contain the following fields: 

{
  "version": "vX.X.X",
  "mode": "Execution",
  "threat_level": 2,
  "authority": "OperatorID",
  "scope": "...",
  "tools_used": [...],
  "artifacts": [...],
  "state_delta": {...},
  "timestamp": "...",
  "hash": "..."
}

Implementations MAY extend this schema but SHALL NOT omit required fields.

4. Validation Rules

An audit record is valid only if:

  • Threat level matches execution classification
  • Authority identifier is present and verifiable
  • All declared tools are recorded
  • State delta reflects actual mutation
  • Artifact references are resolvable

Validation failure invalidates task completion.

5. Failure Conditions

Execution SHALL halt or remain in incomplete state if:

  • Audit write fails
  • Audit integrity hash fails
  • Authority binding mismatch occurs
  • Threat posture mismatch is detected

Incomplete execution MUST NOT be presented as successful.

6. Implementation Mapping

In practical systems, this specification may map to:

  • Immutable append-only logs
  • Signed JSON artifacts
  • Hash-chained state records
  • Versioned execution manifests

Audit persistence SHOULD use tamper-evident storage where feasible.

7. Governance Implications

Audit as completion condition prevents silent execution.

It enforces:

  • Reproducibility
  • Authority accountability
  • Threat scaling proportionality
  • Forensic traceability

Without this principle, governance becomes observational rather than structural.

With it, governance becomes definitional.

AEGIS Specification Node
Operational Layer — Audit Enforcement
